zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Cooper於1485年出生在英國貝德福爾斯特亞特利 煙雨 沙沙時，她的父親Sir John Deol Michael Cowper35歲，母親Joan Stanbridge45歲。她和John Norton至少有12個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1582年3月10日在英國貝德福爾阿爾享年97歲，埋葬在英國艾可沃爾坦 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: Cooper
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker and repairer of wooden vessels such as barrels, tubs, buckets, casks, and vats, from Middle English couper, cowper (apparently from Middle Dutch kūper, a derivative of kūp ‘tub, container’, which was borrowed independently into English as coop). The prevalence of the surname, its cognates, and equivalents bears witness to the fact that this was one of the chief specialist trades in the Middle Ages throughout Europe. In North America, the English surname has absorbed some cases of like-sounding cognates from other languages, for example Dutch Kuiper .
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic) Kupfer and Kupper (see Kuper ).
Dutch: occupational name for a buyer or merchant, Middle Dutch coper.

variants.name: Kuiper
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Hendrik, Willem, Harm, Klaas, Kort, Marinus, Nicolaas, Wouter.
Dutch: occupational name for a cooper, Middle Dutch cup(e)re.

variants.name: Couper
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Cooper  .

variants.name: Hooper
meanings: English (southwestern): occupational name for a cooper, someone who fitted wooden or metal hoops on wooden casks and barrels, or a barrel-maker, from Middle English hoper, an agent derivative of hop ‘hoop, band’. Compare Cooper .

variants.name: Coop
meanings: English (Lancashire): metonymic occupational name for a cooper, from Middle English coupe, cupe ‘tub, cask’ (from Latin cūpa), probably used for a cooper; compare Cooper . In some cases the surname may have been derived from a pub or house sign.
Americanized form of German or Dutch   Koop  .
History: It was David Koop (his father's surname also spelled Kupe) from Germany whose surname was changed to Coop after he settled in PA toward the end of the 18th century. He later moved to VA.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Cooper was born in 1485, in Sharpenhoe, Streatley with Sharpenhoe, Bedfordshire, England, her father, Sir John Deol Michael Cowper, was 35 and her mother, Joan Stanbridge, was 45. She had at least 12 sons and 2 daughters with John Norton. She died on 10 March 1582, in Arlesey, Bedfordshire, England, at the age of 97, and was buried in Waltham Abbey, Essex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Cooperが1485年にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアストレアトリー ウィス シャルペンhオシャルペンhオで生まれた時，父Sir John Deol Michael Cowperは35歳，母Joan Stanbridgeは45歳であった。彼女はJohn Nortonとの間に少なくとも12人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1582年3月10日にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアアルレセイで97歳で死去し，イギリスエセックスワルサム アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.of, Sharpenhoe, Bedfordshire, England: 영국 베드퍼드실르 스트리틀리 위스 샤르펜호 샤르펜호
ko., Sharpenhoe, Bedfordshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 스트리틀리 위스 샤르펜호 샤르펜호
ko.Sharpenhoe, Bedfordshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 스트리틀리 위스 샤르펜호 샤르펜호
ko.Sharpenhoe, Streatley with Sharpenhoe, B: 영국 베드퍼드실르 스트리틀리 위스 샤르펜호 샤르펜호
ko.lifeSummary: Jane Cooper(이)가 1485년 영국 베드퍼드실르 스트리틀리 위스 샤르펜호 샤르펜호에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Deol Michael Cowper(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Stanbridge(은)는 45세였다. 그녀는 John Norton(와)과 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1582년 3월 10일 영국 베드퍼드실르 알레시에서 97세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 에세스 월섬 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Cooper nasceu em 1485, em Sharpenhoe, Streatley with Sharpenhoe, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Deol Michael Cowper, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Joan Stanbridge, tinha 45 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 12 filhos e 2 filhas com John Norton. Ela faleceu em 10 de março de 1582, em Arlesey, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, com 97 anos, e foi sepultada em Waltham Abbey, Essex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Cooper родилась в 1485 г. в местности шарпено, стритли вит шарпено, бедфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Deol Michael Cowper было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Stanbridge, 45. У неё было по крайней мере 12 сыновей и 2 дочери от John Norton. Она умерла 10 марта 1582 г. в местности арльси, бедфордшир, Англия в возрасте 97 лет и была похоронена в местности валтам эбби, эссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Cooper nació en 1485, en Sharpenhoe, Streatley with Sharpenhoe, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Deol Michael Cowper, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Joan Stanbridge, tenía 45 años. Tuvo por lo menos 12 hijos y 2 hijas con John Norton. Murió el 10 de marzo de 1582, en Arlesey, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 97 años, y fue sepultada en Waltham Abbey, Essex, Inglaterra.
